_doc: The capability-module registry — the one home for the kit `setup` offers and the assemblers compose. A capability module is a TOGGLEABLE bundle whose parts live in their existing single homes (a config toggle · per-role prompt FRAGMENTS · optionally a deny rule or quality check); this file catalogs them. A module's FLOOR (the part that is never off) is NOT here — it stays in the role's floor body (src/agents/<role>.md); this registry carries only the DEEPENING (toggle · per-kind defaults · targets · fragment pointers). The assembler (src/adapter/modules.mjs, shared by both install-agents shims) replaces each role's `<!-- ai-dev:modules -->` marker with the ENABLED modules' fragments, in REGISTRY ORDER. HONESTY: a module is `[persona]` prompt content — it sharpens what a role THINKS about; it blocks nothing mechanically unless it also carries a deny rule. Fail-safe enabled-resolution: the `toggle` and `defaults` notes in _row_shape. Adding a module = a row here + its src/modules/<id>/ fragments; the assemblers compose it for free — NO core edit (docs/architecture.md `## Capability modules`).

_row_shape.id: short stable name — matches the src/modules/<id>/ dir and the .ai-dev/config.json modules.<id> toggle key
_row_shape.for: one plain line: what this module gives the project
_row_shape.toggle: the SHAPE of the config value under modules.<id> — e.g. { depth: 'rich | light', nonSecurity: bool }. A literal `false` is the only config-side OFF; any other NAMED value (true, object, malformed) ⇒ ON (fail-safe to more rigor — the config is untrusted input); an ABSENT key falls to `defaults`
_row_shape.defaults: per project kind, the default toggle this module runs with — a literal `false` defaults the module OFF for that kind (registry-authored, our data: the one default-OFF path; any named config value overrides it); unknown/absent kind ⇒ the strict side (docs/architecture.md)
_row_shape.targets: [{ role, beat }] — which role agent the module deepens, at which loop beat (plan | review)
_row_shape.fragments: { <role>: 'src/modules/<id>/<role>.md' } — registry-relative pointers (absolute or `..`-bearing is REJECTED at assembly); the per-role prompt the module composes in

modules.for: Explicit threat enumeration for a security-relevant change — attack surface, data/secret exposure, trust boundaries — deepening the always-on Reviewer security item and adding a plan-time threat pass to the Builder.

Plan-time product discovery for a user-facing change — who it is for, the user pain, whether it is the right bet, the cheapest test, what breaks if it is NOT built — deepening the always-on Builder plan product-question item and the Reviewer's check that the shipped change serves its plan's user claim.

Plan-time test coverage for the layers a unit test cannot reach — integration glue, wiring, a user-visible surface — plus the test-first ratchet on a gate-caught bug; adding a coverage item to the Builder's plan and deepening the Reviewer's Tests item.

Behaviour-truth gates for a non-trivial subsystem — does it do what its name claims, is no failure silently swallowed, does a path parallel to an existing one inherit all its guards — deepening the Reviewer's Hygiene/Correctness/Honesty items and adding a plan-time real-or-marked claim discipline to the Builder.

Usability enumeration for a user-facing surface — adaptivity, accessibility, responsiveness, clarity, adverse states — deepening the Builder's plan-time product questions and the Reviewer's correctness check on the user's actual path.

Source discipline for answering an unknown — the source ladder, recency against the real dependency versions, triangulation, confidence marks — deepening the Builder's unfamiliar-interface (stack-researcher) item and the Reviewer's honesty check on researched claims.

Cause-first fix discipline for a bug — reproduce before fix, discriminating experiments, the cause not the symptom, debuggable-by-design — adding a fix-method item to the Builder's work and deepening the Reviewer's honesty check on 'fixed' claims.

Scale awareness where the change touches a loop, query, or payload over user-scale data — name the scale, bound every path, measure before optimizing — adding the quantity dimension to the Builder's plan and the Reviewer's correctness check.

Data-layer discipline where the change touches a schema or persistent store — migrations, reversibility, rollout compatibility, integrity at the DB — adding the schema dimension to the Builder's plan and the Reviewer's check.

Locale discipline where the project serves (or plans) more than one locale — externalized strings, locale-aware formatting, plural rules, expansion-tolerant layouts — adding the locale dimension to the Builder's plan and the Reviewer's check; inert on a single-locale project.

Interleaving awareness where the change touches shared state, async flows, or parallel execution — name what is shared, atomic check-then-act windows, idempotent retries, ordering — adding to the Builder's plan and the Reviewer's check the dimension a single sequential read-through misses.

Boundary discipline where the change touches a module interface or introduces a new inter-module dependency — name the boundary, check the dependency direction against `docs/architecture.md`, flag missing linter — adding the boundary dimension to the Builder's plan and the Reviewer's correctness check.

Angle-changing inquiry at decision points — a draft brief section, a feature plan, a captured idea — via a compact technique catalog (pre-mortem, inversion, user role-play, persona panel…); entry always offers a depth choice (light default / deeper / skip), one technique per round, never a block. The catalog and the Builder's plan-beat round live here; the interactive offer is the orchestrator's `## Elicitation`.

Adversarial self-probe of the plan draft before build — name the most plausible failure, surface missing scenarios, replace fuzzy criteria, expose hidden structural forks — so the plan's weaknesses are found before the build, not during review.

Concurrent multi-author conflict-avoidance for a collaboration.team:true project — small atomic disjoint-surface PRs, sync-from-main before push, never a blind conflict auto-resolve (re-cut from main / escalate) — deepening the Builder's plan and the Reviewer's review; default OFF everywhere, inert on a single-user project.
